existing.name: Automated RFx template generation
existing.roi: −40% RFx drafting time, from 3 days to <1 day

existing.name: Invoice anomaly detection & auto-match
existing.enabler: PO data quality, IT integration with AP team

existing.name: Supplier risk radar (NLP news monitoring)
existing.enabler: Supplier master data cleanliness, legal sign-off

potential.name: Autonomous should-cost modelling
potential.roi: −25% negotiation prep time, better price anchoring
potential.enabler: BOM data, commodity price feeds, category expertise

potential.name: AI contract obligation extraction & alerting
potential.enabler: Digitised contract repository, legal review workflow

potential.name: Dynamic category strategy co-pilot
potential.enabler: Market intelligence API, structured category data

potential.name: Predictive savings pipeline forecasting
potential.enabler: Historical pipeline data, integration with finance system

techStack.s2p: SAP Ariba + Jaggaer (dual-platform: Ariba for direct, Jaggaer for indirect)
techStack.other: Coupa (T&E), Concur, ServiceNow (IT procurement), Microsoft Copilot (pilots)

general.org-structure: Category-led with 4 clusters: Direct, Indirect, Capex, IT

s-01.q-01-d1-01: Procurement strategy is documented at category level but not yet formally linked to AI transformation roadmap. Board-level visibility exists but KPIs are not AI-specific.
s-01.q-01-d2-01: Core ERP is modern (S/4HANA), good data foundation, but S2P tools are split across two platforms causing integration friction.

s-02.q-02-d1-01: Category management process is well-defined across the main clusters; spend analytics is strong thanks to the Snowflake warehouse.
s-02.q-02-d2-01: AI spend classification is live and performing well at 85% coverage — a genuine competitive advantage vs portfolio peers.

s-04.q-04-d2-02: GPT-4o RFx generation is the most mature AI deployment: fully embedded in buyer workflow, strong adoption, measurable time savings.
s-04.q-04-d3-02: Should-cost AI is identified as high priority but not yet started — represents the biggest near-term AI value unlock.

s-08.q-08-d2-01: Invoice anomaly detection is the highest-ROI live use case. Full coverage of PO invoices with measurable reduction in manual effort.

s-ai.q-ai-d3-01: AI governance is nascent — no formal AI ethics policy, no model risk framework, no AI centre of excellence. High priority gap given the live deployments.
s-ai.q-ai-d3-02: Data privacy and AI vendor management are handled ad hoc. Needs formalisation before scaling further AI use cases.
